topic: A person walking across the Abbey Road zebra crossing, eye-level view.

Shot 3 [00:05-00:08]: The person completes crossing the zebra crossing, eye-level long shot.
parsed_generation.narration: An eye-level sequence follows a person stepping onto the Abbey Road zebra crossing from a front view, then moving with a steady stride in a side-angle. The final long shot captures the person fully crossing, completing the journey across the stripes.

feedback:
The sequence centers on an eye-level crossing of Abbey Road, but the shot structure diverges from the gold standard (three shots with varied timing vs. two 4-second segments) and lacks the canonical static first frame with prominent perspective lines. To better match the gold standard, use two bracketed timestamps of [00:00-00:04] and [00:04-00:08], describe a static eye-level wide shot with perspective lines, then the crossing in center-frame. Also tighten the wording to mirror the concise, cinematic style (no ‘Shot 1/2/3’ labels).

strengths: Clear focus on eye-level perspective and subject crossing.
fixes: Adopt the two-shot structure with uniform 4-second blocks: [00:00-00:04] static eye-level wide shot showing the crossing background; [00:04-00:08] subject stepping onto stripes toward the center.
Use bracketed timestamps only (e.g., [00:00-00:04]) and remove explicit 'Shot 1/2/3' labeling.
